title: Inventory satellite dust-detection, ground PM10, dust-contaminant, and respiratory health data sources for LMIC arid regions
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite dust-detection products (MODIS AOD, CALIOP, CAMS), ground PM10 monitors, dust-contaminant characterization studies, and respiratory health-utilization data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC arid-region coverage assessment.
inputs: MODIS AOD product documentation and desert-surface validation studies
WHO air-quality database monitor inventory for arid LMIC countries
Published dust-contaminant characterization studies by source region
Respiratory health-utilization data availability from LMIC HMIS
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, spatial resolution, and documented limitations — particularly AOD accuracy over bright surfaces and monitor sparsity in arid LMIC regions.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, LMIC arid-region coverage, AOD accuracy limitations, and monitor-sparsity documentation.

title: Map dust-storm PM10 exposure from satellite AOD and CAMS forecasts for at least two LMIC arid regions
outcome: A sub-national dust-exposure analysis for at least two LMIC arid regions using satellite AOD and CAMS dust-forecast data, documenting AOD-to-ground-PM10 transfer methodology, bright-surface limitations, and ground-monitor sparsity.
inputs: MODIS AOD data for target regions
CAMS dust-forecast output for target regions
Ground PM10 monitor data where available
Boundary-layer height and meteorological data for AOD-to-PM10 transfer
expected_artifact: validation.md update with dust-exposure methodology and regional results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms AOD data extraction, dust-exposure classification methodology, and bright-surface limitation documentation, with explicit validation against ground monitors where available.
done_condition: Dust-exposure analysis includes methodology, regional results, AOD-to-PM10 transfer limitations, bright-surface documentation, and replication status for at least two regions.

title: Analyze respiratory health impact of dust-specific PM10 with confound controls for non-dust sources
outcome: A health-impact analysis linking dust-specific PM10 exposure to respiratory outcomes for at least one LMIC arid region, with explicit confound controls for non-dust PM10 sources and dust-source toxicology documentation.
inputs: respiratory health-utilization data from LMIC HMIS
non-dust PM10 baseline data where available
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with health-impact methodology and confound documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms health-impact methodology, confound-control approach, and dust-toxicology documentation for each target region.
done_condition: Health-impact analysis includes methodology, confound controls for at least two non-dust PM10 sources, dust-toxicology context, and replication status.

title: Red-team dust-exposure misclassification and health-impact overstatement risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how satellite dust-exposure estimates could misclassify exposure over bright surfaces, how health-impact estimates could overstate effects by ignoring non-dust confounders, and how differential-toxicity claims could be premature.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every health-impact claim has exposure-validation limitations, confound controls, and dust-toxicology caveats. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every dust-health claim is either blocked, softened to exposure-misclassification-limited language with explicit confound and toxicology caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match air-quality management and health-ministry decision needs in arid LMIC regions
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the dust-health analysis would change any real decision for air-quality management agencies, health ministries, or land-degradation programs in arid LMIC regions without creating perverse incentives.
inputs: air-quality management context for arid regions
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for air-quality and health-ministry audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that dust-health estimates are used to justify land-degradation interventions without evidence of health-effectiveness, or that bright-surface AOD limitations are ignored. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Niger Ministry of Environment dust-early-warning officer), named decision (e.g., dust-event health advisory activation), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for land-degradation intervention justification without health-effectiveness evidence), and replicated field-reality review.
